source: Faz12 master-harness 2nd pass — 6 parallel Claude general-purpose sub-agents over the breadth the Faz11 fleet under-covered (server uncovered, orchestration/bin + bin/lib, bin/ bridges, src/ frontend remainder, scripts). Dedup-aware vs the 39 prior findings + 6 fixed. Tier-1 spot-verified the security + CRITICAL ones; rest = agent-reproduced (CONFIRMED) or code-read (SUSPECTED).

findings.name: wrong claim-ledger path → collision-avoidance dead
findings.symptom: CLAIMS='orchestration/claims.jsonl' but the real ledger is seyir/work-claim.jsonl (lib/claims defaultStore). readActiveClaims() always reads [] → heartbeat re-picks work other tabs already claimed (collision-safe action pick is a permanent silent no-op).
findings.fix: const CLAIMS = defaultStore(seyirDir).ledgerPath (seyir/work-claim.jsonl).
findings.evidence: Agent: seyir/work-claim.jsonl exists (3.6k), orchestration/claims.jsonl does NOT.

findings.name: restored Firebase session never clears needsAuth/token
findings.symptom: onAuthStateChanged currentUser branch only setUser(currentUser); never setToken or setNeedsAuth(false). A returning user with a persisted session is stuck on the 'Sign in with Google' screen forever; Google Drive never loads.
findings.fix: In the currentUser branch fetch a fresh OAuth token (getAccessToken) and setToken + setNeedsAuth(false).
findings.evidence: Tier-1: handleLogin (useAuth.ts:41) DOES setNeedsAuth(false); firebase.ts cachedAccessToken is set ONLY at googleSignIn (Google Drive OAuth token is NOT persisted by Firebase; reload→null), so a restored session showing sign-in + one-click re-consent is BY DESIGN (a popup can't open without a user gesture → silent auto-sign-in is impossible). 'stuck forever' is false.
findings.verdict: REJECTED (Tier-1 refuted: by-design re-consent, not a bug)

findings.name: hardware calibration JSON.parse always throws → dead
findings.symptom: JSON.parse(rawOutput.replace(/'/g,'"')) on Python print(dict) output — Python emits False/True/None (not JSON) and apostrophe-swap corrupts strings; catch swallows it → calibration permanently returns {profile:'Standard'}.
findings.fix: calibrate_hardware.py should print json.dumps(...); parse raw (no quote-swap).
findings.evidence: Agent: python3 -c "print({'gpu_available':False})" → JSON.parse CRASH 'Unexpected token F'.

findings.name: safeWritePath no realpath → symlink escapes BRIDGE_WRITE_ROOTS
findings.symptom: path.resolve collapses ../ but does NOT resolve symlinks; a symlink inside an allowed root pointing outside (<root>/sub/evil -> /etc) passes startsWith(root) → write escapes confinement. (auth-gated: caller needs bridge HMAC/token, so defense-in-depth not remote-RCE.)
findings.fix: fs.realpathSync the parent dir before the startsWith check.
findings.evidence: Agent reproduced: /tmp/stproot/sub/evil/passwd (evil→/etc) → ok:true. Tier-1 read bridge-guard.mjs:24-31 (resolve+startsWith, no realpath).

findings.name: bridge token compare not constant-time
findings.symptom: req.headers['x-bridge-token'] === TOKEN is a non-constant-time compare → timing side-channel on the bridge token (the HMAC path already uses timingSafeEqual).
findings.fix: crypto.timingSafeEqual over equal-length buffers.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read terminal-bridge.mjs:224.

findings.name: /read HMAC verifies over empty path/body → HMAC-only clients 401
findings.symptom: /read calls authed(req) with no raw/bridgePath, so HMAC verification runs over body='' path='' and never matches a real client signature → every HMAC-only client (server with no token) gets 401 on /read; bridgeRead/health_probe term snapshot dead when only HOST_BRIDGE_HMAC_SECRET is set.
findings.fix: authed(req, '', '/read') with proper GET method/path canonicalization.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read; HMAC branch signs over /read but verify uses ''.

findings.name: conduct detail matches table header, never the action
findings.symptom: detailFor('conduct') matches the FIRST CONDUCTOR.md line containing → — that's the summary table header '| Lane | Şu an | → Sıradaki |' — so the conduct step ALWAYS reports column labels, never the real next-action.
findings.fix: Anchor on the action block (/^\*\*Eylem|🎯/), not bare →.
findings.evidence: Agent: CONDUCTOR.md line 7 (table header) is the first → match.

findings.name: empty kindPattern suppresses ALL findings
findings.symptom: loadSuppress accepts a rule with kindPattern:'' (only typeof check, not non-empty); applySuppress does f.kind.includes('') → always true → one such rule silently suppresses ALL findings of that detector, defeating no-silent-cap.
findings.fix: add && r.kindPattern.trim().length>0 to the filter.
findings.evidence: Agent: 'anything'.includes('')===true; loadFilter accepts kindPattern:''.

findings.name: self_analyzer tool unreachable
findings.symptom: executeTool('self_analyzer') is unreachable — self_analyzer is NOT in tools.json.tools, so the if(!tool) guard (line 36) returns 'Registration not found' before name-dispatch; SystemAnalyzer.analyze() never runs via orchestrator.
findings.fix: Add self_analyzer registry entry, or move name-dispatch before registry lookup.
findings.evidence: Agent verified: self_analyzer in registry === false.

findings.name: graceful-skeleton catch crashes on bad url → no bundle
findings.symptom: the 'always produce a bundle' catch-block calls new URL(undefined) on a source with missing url → TypeError INSIDE the catch → propagates out of draft()→run() → NO bundle written (violates the file's core guarantee).
findings.fix: wrap hostname extraction in try, or filter valid urls first.
findings.evidence: Agent: node -e 'new URL(undefined)' → TypeError; research():52 can return undefined url.

findings.name: consent/leave/cpu-cap handlers are dead (no backend call)
findings.symptom: handleConsent/handleLeave only set local state, never POST /api/cluster/consent or /api/cluster/leave; the CPU-cap slider never POSTs numCtxLimit to /api/cluster/config → Join/Leave Mesh + resource governor have zero backend effect (routes exist at server.ts:1913/1925/1897).
findings.fix: await api.post('/api/cluster/consent',{approved:true}) etc.; debounced /api/cluster/config for cpuCap.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read ClusterManager.tsx:14-22 (no api.* call); server routes exist.

findings.name: OAuth token never refreshed → Drive 401 with no recovery
findings.symptom: fetchFiles effect depends only on [token]; the Google OAuth accessToken expires (~1h) and is never refreshed → every Drive GET/DELETE 401s with no recovery; user sees a generic error.
findings.fix: Catch 401 → trigger re-auth/refresh before the call.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read; firebase.ts getAccessToken returns cached token, no refresh.

findings.name: tool-gap report 100% false positives
findings.symptom: gaps filter flags ALL tools as missing because tool names (desktop_commander) never equal script basenames (calibrate_hardware.py); the gap report is pure noise.
findings.fix: match against tool.entryPoint/script field, not name+'.py'.
findings.evidence: Agent reproduced: 22/22 tools flagged as gaps.

findings.name: localExecution returns fake 'Simulation Success'
findings.symptom: localExecution never executes — builds a command string, logs it, returns output:'Simulation Success'; surrounding try/catch is dead; every non-MCP/API/named tool silently returns fake success.
findings.fix: invoke the binary or return an explicit not-implemented error.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read: body synchronous, no exec/await.

findings.name: readFileSync cwd-relative, no guard → ENOENT crash
findings.symptom: fs.readFileSync('tools.json') uses a cwd-relative path with no try/catch → throws ENOENT and crashes the caller when cwd != repo root.
findings.fix: resolve via path.join(__dirname,...) + guard.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read; orchestrator.ts:14 uses process.cwd() for same file, analyzer doesn't.

findings.name: loadManifest permanently caches null
findings.symptom: loadManifest caches null if manifest.json absent on first call (boot before make build-all); discoverBinaries then reports 0 binaries forever (no fs fallback unlike resolveBinary).
findings.fix: cache only successful reads, or re-stat on null.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read: cache!==undefined short-circuits retries.

findings.name: S3 upload uses deprecated SigV2 auth → 401/403
findings.symptom: S3 upload sets Authorization: AWS <access>:<secret> (SigV2) which real S3/MinIO rejects; non-presigned uploads 401/403 though the path is advertised as working.
findings.fix: use SigV4 or document presigned-URL-only.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read; comment admits 'requires complex crypto signing'.

findings.name: serverUp() curl no --fail → 500 reported up
findings.symptom: serverUp() runs curl without --fail/-f; curl exits 0 on HTTP 500/404 as long as TCP connects → reports server up while /api/health errors, letting runHeal proceed on a broken server.
findings.fix: add -f to curl args (or check status).
findings.evidence: Agent: curl -s -o /dev/null returns 0 for any HTTP status without --fail.

findings.name: git ahead/behind swapped + mislabeled
findings.symptom: git rev-list --left-right --count @{u}...HEAD outputs <behind>TAB<ahead>; .replace shows 'behind/ahead' but header is ↑/↓ and legend says 'ahead/behind' → values swapped/mislabeled.
findings.fix: swap to HEAD...@{u} or relabel column.
findings.evidence: Agent: git left=@{u}-only=behind, right=HEAD-only=ahead.

findings.name: auditDuplication overlap inflated (array not deduped)
findings.symptom: b = keywords(...).filter(...) is an array NOT deduped; overlap=b.filter(k=>a.has(k)).length counts repeated words → inflated overlap → false 'duplicate' Gap when one purpose repeats a distinctive word.
findings.fix: wrap b in a Set like a.
findings.evidence: Agent: a=Set{alpha,bravo}, b=[alpha,alpha] → overlap=2, ratio=1.0.

findings.name: default kill (no --sig) → target undefined → broken
findings.symptom: with no --sig, sigIdx=-1 so filter i!==sigIdx+1 drops index 0 → target becomes undefined → throws 'target required'; the registry builder omits --sig by default, so default kills are broken.
findings.fix: drop only when sigIdx>=0 && i===sigIdx+1.
findings.evidence: Agent reproduced: pickTarget(['1234']) → undefined.

findings.name: MCP SSE parse breaks → builtin-count invariant silently skipped
findings.symptom: r.body.slice(r.body.indexOf('{')) + JSON.parse on a text/event-stream body (data: {...}\n\n multi-frame / trailing event:/id: lines) fails, caught silently → the builtin>=29 invariant check silently degrades to SKIP, never asserting.
findings.fix: strip the 'data: ' prefix and parse the first SSE frame only.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read; slice-to-end includes trailing framing that breaks JSON.parse.

findings.name: jobId collision (Math.random base36)
findings.symptom: Math.random().toString(36).substring(7) jobId (~36^5) collides; a collision overwrites a live job's status entry in JOB_STORE.
findings.fix: crypto.randomUUID().
findings.evidence: Agent reproduced: 5 collisions / 100k IDs.

findings.name: JOB_STORE unbounded memory leak
findings.symptom: completed/failed jobs never evicted from JOB_STORE Map → long-running process leaks memory.
findings.fix: TTL/LRU eviction on completion.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read: no delete/cap.

findings.name: backup salt is dead (not in key derivation)
findings.symptom: salt = randomBytes(16) prepended to the blob and re-read on restore, but NEVER fed into key derivation (key is raw masterKey); the 'zero-knowledge derive from salt' comment is false.
findings.fix: drop salt or actually HKDF the key from it.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read: restore reads salt then ignores it.

findings.name: FileReader no onerror → restore silently frozen
findings.symptom: FileReader has onload but no onerror; choosing a corrupt/unreadable .enc file silently does nothing — no notify, loading never toggles, restore appears frozen.
findings.fix: reader.onerror = () => onNotify('Failed to read backup file','error').
findings.evidence: Agent code-read BackupControl.tsx:82-107 (only onload).

findings.name: process_port ok always true
findings.symptom: lsof ... || echo 'no listener' always exits 0 → ok:r.exitCode===0 always true even when nothing listens (the listening flag is the only real signal).
findings.fix: derive ok from listening, not exitCode.
findings.evidence: Agent code-read: || echo masks lsof exit 1.

findings.name: suppress count breakdown vs total mismatch
findings.symptom: stderr breakdown uses pre-suppress raw counts (lapsesR*.length) while total ${lapses.length} is post-suppress → per-category sum can exceed the total when any lapse is suppressed (cosmetic).
findings.fix: compute per-rule counts from filtered lapses (like critic.ts byKind).
findings.evidence: Agent code-read dod.ts:88 (post) vs :107 (pre).

findings.name: missing title → '# undefined' output
findings.symptom: draft() only validates obj.body_markdown; an LLM reply omitting title yields post.md header '# undefined' and seo.json with undefined fields.
findings.fix: default d.title/seo fields to topic.
findings.evidence: Agent reproduced: '# ${({body_markdown:'x'}).title}' → '# undefined'.

findings.name: sender mask greedy → fragments counts
findings.symptom: sender masking /\+.*@/ is greedy and keys bySender on the full 'Display <addr>' string; a sender with a '+' before an unrelated '@' mangles the key, fragmenting frequency/rising counts.
findings.fix: anchor mask to local-part: /\+[^@]*@/.
findings.evidence: Agent: 'a+x@b, c@d'.replace(/\+.*@/,'@') → 'a@d' (over-eats).
